raw_title: Pariah "Pariah - Unity / Clear/Red Splatter LP" 12"
page_text_snippet: Pariah's "Unity" re-issued! Satan released this album under the name Pariah in 1997! Thrash Metal attack! First time on vinyl. "Unity" was the comeback album of Pariah/Satan, released after 9 years after they split-up, in 1997. Released on the band's own label Aartee Music, and only 1.000 copies were made on CD. Pariah (Satan) is a band that did change much over the years, evolving at lightning speed, so was a bit of a surprise that "Unity" did not sound drastically different to their previous albums. They sound perhaps a tiny notch softer and less aggressive than before and this is largely because Alan Hunter (Tysondog) possessing a softer and more traditional voice, reminiscent of Joe Elliott from Def Leppard - has replaced Michael Jackson as vocalist. "Unity" doesn't disappoint as Ramsey and Tippins exchange riffs and solos as briskly as before and the consistent and strong songwriting of "Blaze of Obscurity" is present here also. Broadly speaking, this still works in the realm of w

page_text_snippet: Skymning's "Stormchoirs" remastered and first time on vinyl. Top notch melodic Swedish Death Metal! Melodic Power/Death Metal? Yes, it does sound strange, but we believe that this "label" fits perfectly into the music because these guys have the skills in mixing these apparently "so far apart genres" and create an excellent album, full of melodies and supported by a heavy base of extreme metal. A gem from the Invasion Records roster, Skymning arrived onto the Swedish melodic Death Metal scene with an even more pronounced reliance on the former half of that genre. Essentially, they were writing power metal tunes and slathering the sorts of growled vocals you'd expect form their peers right on top of them.Certainly, bands like a Dark Tranquillity or In Flames could also be equated to using traditional, melodic Metal aesthetics, but this group took it to a whole new level on their debut. page_text_snippet: With "My Body to the Worms", Mammon's Throne bends the sub-genres of metal to their will to forge a crushing offering of dark extreme metal! After relentless gigging and the success of their 2020 debut album "Forward Unto Flame", Mammon's Throne have established themselves as a force to be reckoned within the Australian live music scene. They then followed this up with an epic offering of extreme doom on their self-titled release in 2023. This album saw the band fully embrace the death-doom and black metal aspects of their sound and was met with great admiration by listeners and reviewers. On the back of the strength of their live performances and respect within the local music community, the band has supported multiple international touring acts such as My Dying Bride, Pallbearer, Conan, Triumph of Death and Rotting Christ to name a few and has done a run of shows in Japan in 2024. page_text_snippet: Forgotten Swedish Death Metal album "The Creation" finally available on vinyl! Sins of Omission was a great Swedish band trailing in the footsteps of the formidable At the Gates, and one could say that they probably had a sound close to the "Slaughter of the Soul" era, with a few pretty notable exceptions which push their 1999 debut over the top from just a trend hopper to something well worth having. From the stunning Kristian Wåhlin artwork to the excellent punch of the production, this was for sure one of the better efforts heard from the tenure of Sweden's Black Sun Records, a label with some promise that unfortunately did not survive very far into the new millennium. But that is clearly no fault of this quartet, who had much of the promise of their own influences but never broke to a broader audience, and we know it sounds like a broken record but it was all due to the saturation of this sound. raw_title: Pungent Stench "Pungent Stench - For God Your Soul…For Me Your Flesh / Bold/Black Splatter Vinyl LP" 12"
page_text_snippet: Pure filthy Death Metal perfection! Pungent Stench's 1990 classic debut remastered! The year 1990 was the moment Death Metal truly exploded. Bands like Entombed released their debut full lengths (which would later go on to be some of the most respected albums of the entire genre) and soon to be giants of the genre Deicide and Cannibal Corpse would also release their first albums. But in Austria something even stranger and more twisted was festering. Pungent Stench arrived with "For God Your Soul… For Me Your Flesh", a debut that smashed Death Metal and early Grindcore together into a sound that sounded like a cross between Obituary and Terrorizer! What makes this record stand out is its total rejection of finesse. No melody. No clean lines. No attempt at showing off. Just a filthy, hostile churn of riffs that somehow stick in your skull long after the noise dies down. Tracks like "A Small Lunch" and "Blood, Pus and Gastric Juice" prove that primitive Death Metal always hits hardest whe

title: Embalming / Black Smoke Vinyl LP" 12"
raw_title: Heteropsy "Heteropsy - Embalming / Black Smoke Vinyl LP" 12"
page_text_snippet: From the shadows of Japan's underground: guttural HM-2 Doom/Death Metal! Tokyo based Death/Doom Metal band Heteropsy was formed in 2020 after Swedish-style Death Metal band Frostvore went into hibernation. Ex-Frostvore members Shigenori Tamura and Koki Fukushima started a new Doom Metal project. After several lineup changes, the band solidified with Kota Maruyama and Hiroki Sato. Heteropsy actively perform live shows mainly in Tokyo while also composing new material. In 2025 they recorded their first album "Embalming" at Void)))Lab, Tokyo, with engineer Ryuhi Inari (known for work with Coffins and Church of Misery). Together they created a sound endlessly dark, rugged, dusky yet mournful, featuring the legendary HM-2 chainsaw tone. "Embalming" introduces Heteropsy's styles from the Far East to the world: a mix of old school Death Metal (Autopsy, Dismember, Rippikoulu, Coffins), sometimes Doom, sometimes horns up, sometimes ambient, always with a melancholic and soulful edge. — Tracklis

page_text_snippet: Lane Shi Otay Onii, known professionally as Otay:Onii, is one of the most inquisitive and genre-defying artists of her generation. A multifaceted creator whose work spans performance art, composition, installation, and avant-garde music practice. Otay:Onii is the first ever artist selected for the three-year Triennium Residency at Roadburn Festival. She was a contributing composer and performer in Florentina Holzinger's groundbreaking opera production ‘ SANCTA ‘ and has performed at festivals world-wide such as SXSW, Roadburn, CTM, Fusion, Creepy Teepy, LGW, Fekete Zaj, and Full of Lava . Otay;Onii has already been celebrated with many awards worldwide, included the Global Music Awards ‘Bronze Prize for Best Female Vocalist‘ and ‘Best Sound' at Audiovisual Arts Industrial Incubator Awards. Pelagic records are proud to unveil Otay:Onii's new album ‘Love is in the Shit', a deeply intimate album that reaches a striking balance between control and chaos.
